subject: Chemistry
chapter: Organic Chemistry: Some Basic Principles and Techniques

questions.q: What is the main difference between a primary, secondary, and tertiary alcohol?
questions.answer: Primary alcohols have one alkyl group attached to the carbon atom with the hydroxyl (-OH) group. Secondary alcohols have two alkyl groups attached, while tertiary alcohols have three alkyl groups attached to the carbon atom with the hydroxyl group.

questions.q: Explain the structural isomerism in alkanes.
questions.answer: Structural isomerism in alkanes occurs when two or more molecules have the same molecular formula but differ in the arrangement of their atoms. This type of isomerism is also known as chain isomerism.

questions.q: What is the difference between a functional group and a homologous series?
questions.answer: A functional group is a specific group of atoms within a molecule that determines its chemical properties. A homologous series is a group of compounds that have the same functional group and differ only in the length of their carbon chain.

questions.q: What is the process of fractional distillation?
questions.answer: Fractional distillation is a technique used to separate a mixture of liquids based on their boiling points. It involves heating the mixture and collecting the fractions of the liquids as they vaporize and condense at different temperatures.
questions.topic: Methods of Purification of Organic Compounds

questions.q: What is the difference between an acid and a base in organic chemistry?
questions.answer: In organic chemistry, acids are substances that donate a proton (H+ ion) and bases are substances that accept a proton. This is known as the Bronsted-Lowry theory.

questions.q: Explain the concept of optical isomerism.
questions.answer: Optical isomerism occurs when a molecule and its mirror image cannot be superimposed on each other. This is due to the presence of a chiral center in the molecule, which is an atom attached to four different groups.

questions.q: What is the purpose of the Grignard reaction?
questions.answer: The Grignard reaction is a method used to synthesize alcohols and other organic compounds. It involves the reaction of a Grignard reagent with a carbonyl compound to produce a new carbon-carbon bond.

questions.q: Explain the concept of resonance in organic chemistry.
questions.answer: Resonance is a phenomenon where a molecule can exist in multiple forms, each with a different arrangement of electrons. This is represented by resonance structures, which show the different possible arrangements of electrons in the molecule.

questions.q: What is the difference between an epoxide and an alkene?
questions.answer: An epoxide is a cyclic ether with a three-membered ring, while an alkene is an unsaturated hydrocarbon with a carbon-carbon double bond. The main difference between the two is the presence of the oxygen atom in the epoxide ring.

Crystallization is a method used to purify a solid compound. It involves dissolving the compound in a solvent and then slowly cooling the solution, allowing the compound to crystallize out of the solution.
questions.topic: Methods of Purification of Organic Compounds
